proper_shipping_name: Gas oil or Diesel fuel or Heating oil, light
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E for all carriage; the D part applies to tanker/bulk carriage (forbidden through D and E). Parentheses: applies only above the 1000-point threshold.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1202 (Class 3, PG III, (D/E)); cross-checked adrdangerousgoods.com UN1202

proper_shipping_name: Motor spirit or Gasoline or Petrol
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E for all carriage; the D part applies to tanker/bulk carriage (forbidden through D and E).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1203

proper_shipping_name: Ethanol (Ethyl alcohol) or Ethanol solution (Ethyl alcohol solution)
tunnel_code_meaning: UN1170 has multiple ADR Table A entries (ethanol PG II tunnel code (D/E); ethanol solution PG II/III tunnel code (D/E)); packing group is concentration/entry-dependent and not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1170 (multiple entries: ethanol PG II; ethanol solution PG II/III); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1170

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1263 has multiple ADR Table A entries spanning PG I, II and III with differing tunnel codes (PG I/II/III all (D/E) plus viscous and high-vapour-pressure variants); packing group and exact tunnel code depend on the specific entry, not the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1263 (multiple Class 3 entries, PG I/II/III)

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1993 has multiple ADR Table A entries spanning PG I, II and III (e.g. PG II tunnel code (D/E)); as a generic n.o.s. entry the packing group and tunnel code are assigned per the specific substance and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1993 (multiple entries PG I/II/III; PG II Transport Cat 2, (D/E)); nextsds.com UN1993

tunnel_code_meaning: No tunnel restriction assigned in ADR Table A (column 15 empty).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3082 (Class 9, PG III, no tunnel code); adrdangerousgoods.com UN3082

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1950 has many ADR Table A entries by hazard subtype (flammable, corrosive, toxic, oxidizing, asphyxiant, etc.) with differing tunnel codes (e.g. (D) for several, others varying); the tunnel code depends on the specific aerosol entry and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone. Aerosols (Class 2) have no packing group.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1950 (multiple Class 2 aerosol entries); adr-tool.com UN1950 variants

proper_shipping_name: Lithium ion batteries (including lithium ion polymer batteries)
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E only (most permissive restriction). Parentheses: applies above the 1000-point threshold. Transport Category 2.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3480 (Class 9, no PG, (E), Transport Cat 2, SP 188/230/310/348/376/377/387/636); freightutils.com ADR 2025 UN3480

proper_shipping_name: Lithium ion batteries contained in equipment or Lithium ion batteries packed with equipment (including lithium ion polymer batteries)
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E only. Parentheses: applies above the 1000-point threshold. Transport Category 2.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3481 (Class 9, no PG, (E), SP 188/230/310/348/360/376/377/387/636); adr-tool.com UN3481

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E only. UN1789 has both PG II and PG III entries; packing group depends on concentration and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1789 (Class 8, PG II and PG III variants, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1789

proper_shipping_name: Sulphuric acid with more than 51% acid
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E only.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1830 (Class 8, PG II, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1830

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E for all carriage; the D part applies to tanker/bulk carriage (forbidden through D and E).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1219 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1219

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: passage forbidden through tunnel category E for all carriage; the D part applies to tanker/bulk carriage (forbidden through D and E).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1090 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1090

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1133 has multiple ADR Table A entries spanning PG I, II and III with differing tunnel codes (PG I/II/III all (D/E) plus viscous/high-vapour-pressure variants); packing group and tunnel code depend on the specific entry, not the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1133 (multiple Class 3 entries, PG I/II/III)

tunnel_code_meaning: No tunnel restriction assigned in ADR Table A (column 15 empty).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3077 (Class 9, PG III, no tunnel code); adrdangerousgoods.com UN3077

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through category E for all carriage; D applies to tanker/bulk. UN1230 carries a 6.1 toxic subsidiary hazard (3 (6.1)).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1230 (Class 3 (6.1), PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1230

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through category E for all carriage; D applies to tanker/bulk.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1294 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1294

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1866 has multiple ADR Table A entries spanning PG I, II and III (viscous and vapour-pressure variants), all Class 3 with tunnel code (D/E). Packing group and limited quantity depend on the specific flash-point/viscosity entry and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone; where resolved the tunnel code is D/E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1866 (multiple Class 3 entries, PG I/II/III, (D/E)); adr-tool.com / adrdangerousgoods.com UN1866

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. entry. UN1760 has ADR Table A entries for PG I, II and III, all Class 8 with tunnel code (E). Packing group and limited quantity depend on the specific corrosive substance and cannot be determined from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1760 (multiple Class 8 entries PG I/II/III, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1760

proper_shipping_name: Batteries, wet, filled with acid, electric storage
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only (applies above the 1000-point threshold). No packing group is assigned to this Class 8 article entry.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2794 (Class 8, no PG, (E), SP 295/598); adrdangerousgoods.com UN2794

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. UN1824 has PG II and PG III entries depending on concentration; packing group and limited quantity are concentration-dependent and need the specific solution strength to confirm.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1824 (Class 8, PG II and III variants, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1824

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. entry. UN2811 has ADR Table A entries for PG I, II and III, all Class 6.1; tunnel code is (D) for PG I/II variants and (E) for PG III. Packing group, tunnel code and limited quantity depend on the specific toxic substance and cannot be determined from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2811 (multiple Class 6.1 entries PG I/II/III); adr-tool.com UN2811

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1888 (Class 6.1, PG III, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1888

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through categories B, C, D and E for bulk/tanker carriage; D part forbidden through D and E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; gases have no packing group. Specific mixture letter (A, A01, A02, A0, A1, B1, B2, B, C) affects details but the road tunnel code is B/D.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1965 (Class 2.1, (B/D), Transport Cat 2); adrdangerousgoods.com / nextsds.com UN1965

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable gas with 5.1 oxidizing subsidiary hazard (2.2 (5.1)); gases have no packing group.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1072 (Class 2.2 (5.1), (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1072

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic gas; gases have no packing group. Transport Category 3.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1066 (Class 2.2, (E), Transport Cat 3); adrdangerousgoods.com / nextsds.com UN1066

proper_shipping_name: Ammonium nitrate with not more than 0.2% combustible substances, including any organic substance calculated as carbon, to the exclusion of any other added substance
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 5.1 oxidizer; special provisions restrict composition. Other ammonium nitrate UN numbers (0222, 2067, 2426, 3375) exist for different forms — confirm the cargo matches the <=0.2% combustibles definition.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1942 (Class 5.1, PG III, (E)); adr-tool.com / adrbook.com UN1942

proper_shipping_name: Hydrogen peroxide, aqueous solution with not less than 20% but not more than 60% hydrogen peroxide (stabilized as necessary)
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 5.1 oxidizer with 8 corrosive subsidiary hazard (5.1 (8)), PG II, Transport Category 2.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2014 (Class 5.1 (8), PG II, (E)); nextsds.com UN2014

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. entry. UN1325 has ADR Table A entries for PG II and PG III, both Class 4.1 with tunnel code (E). Packing group and limited quantity depend on the specific flammable solid and cannot be determined from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1325 (Class 4.1, PG II/III, (E)); chemydata.com / nextsds.com UN1325

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. UN1791 has PG II and PG III entries depending on concentration; packing group and limited quantity are concentration-dependent and need the specific solution strength to confirm.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1791 (Class 8, PG II and III variants, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1791

proper_shipping_name: Carbon dioxide, solid or Dry ice
tunnel_code_meaning: No tunnel restriction assigned in ADR Table A. UN1845 is a Class 9 entry with no packing group; carriage as a refrigerant/coolant is governed by special provisions (e.g. SP 593, 663) rather than full ADR for many uses.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1845 (Class 9, no PG, no tunnel code, SP 593/663); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1845

proper_shipping_name: Lithium metal batteries (including lithium alloy batteries)
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only (most permissive; applies above the 1000-point threshold). Class 9 article entry, no packing group. Transport Category 2; SP 188/230/310/376/377/387/636 apply.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3090 (Class 9, no PG, (E)); adr-tool.com UN3090

proper_shipping_name: Lithium metal batteries contained in equipment or Lithium metal batteries packed with equipment (including lithium alloy batteries)
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 9 article entry, no packing group. SP 188/230/310/360/376/377/387/636 apply.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3091 (Class 9, no PG, (E)); adr-tool.com UN3091

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/E: E applies to all carriage (forbidden through category E); B applies to tanker/bulk carriage (forbidden through categories B, C, D and E). Class 4.3 water-reactive; reacts with water to release acetylene. UN1402 also has a PG II entry; PG I is the higher-hazard form.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1402 (Class 4.3, PG I, (B/E); also PG II variant); adrdangerousgoods.com UN1402

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 4.3 water-reactive solid (reacts vigorously with water); PG I.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1428 (Class 4.3, PG I, (E)); adr-tool.com UN1428

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. entry. UN3088 has ADR Table A entries for PG II (tunnel code (D/E)) and PG III (tunnel code (E)), both Class 4.2 self-heating. Packing group, tunnel code and limited quantity depend on the specific substance and cannot be determined from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3088 (Class 4.2, PG II (D/E) and PG III (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN3088

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1361 has ADR Table A entries for PG II (tunnel code (D/E)) and PG III (tunnel code (E)), both Class 4.2 self-heating. Packing group, tunnel code and limited quantity depend on the specific carbon form and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1361 (Class 4.2, PG II and III); 49 CFR HMT cross-reference (Class 4.2, PG II/III)

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D: forbidden through categories D and E. Class 5.2 organic peroxide; organic peroxides are not assigned packing groups (type letter B-F denotes hazard instead). Type D is not temperature-controlled (distinct from UN3115).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3105 (Class 5.2, no PG, (D)); adrdangerousgoods.com UN3105

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 6.1 toxic substance, PG III.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1593 (Class 6.1, PG III, (E)); adr-tool.com UN1593

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. entry. UN2810 has ADR Table A entries for PG I, II and III, all Class 6.1; tunnel code is (C/D) or (D) for higher packing groups and (E) for PG III, and some PG I inhalation-hazard variants carry stricter codes. Packing group, tunnel code and limited quantity depend on the specific toxic substance and cannot be determined from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2810 (multiple Class 6.1 entries PG I/II/III); adr-tool.com UN2810

tunnel_code_meaning: UN2031 has multiple ADR Table A entries by concentration: red fuming and high-concentration forms carry an 8 (5.1) oxidizing subsidiary hazard and PG I with tunnel code (C/D) or (C/E); other-than-red-fuming forms are PG II (tunnel code (E)). Packing group, subsidiary hazard, tunnel code and limited quantity depend on concentration and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2031 (Class 8, multiple entries PG I/II, some with 5.1 subsidiary)

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1267 has ADR Table A entries for PG I, II and III (and a high-vapour-pressure variant), all Class 3 with tunnel code (D/E) where resolved (forbidden through E for all carriage, D for tanker/bulk). Packing group and limited quantity depend on flash-point/vapour pressure and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1267 (multiple Class 3 entries PG I/II/III, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com / nextsds.com UN1267

proper_shipping_name: Petroleum distillates, n.o.s. or Petroleum products, n.o.s.
tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. entry. UN1268 has ADR Table A entries for PG I, II and III, all Class 3 with tunnel code (D/E) where resolved. Packing group and limited quantity depend on the specific product flash-point/vapour pressure and cannot be determined from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1268 (multiple Class 3 entries PG I/II/III); adr-tool.com / hazmattool.com UN1268

proper_shipping_name: Elevated temperature liquid, n.o.s., at or above 100 C and below its flash-point (including molten metals, molten salts, etc.)
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D: forbidden through categories D and E. Class 9 entry, PG III; the elevated-temperature hazard (carried at or above 100 C) drives the restriction rather than a chemical hazard.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3257 (Class 9, PG III, (D)); adr-tool.com UN3257

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1863 has ADR Table A entries for PG I, II and III (with vapour-pressure/flash-point variants), all Class 3 with tunnel code (D/E) where resolved (forbidden through E for all carriage, D for tanker/bulk). Packing group and limited quantity depend on the specific fuel grade and cannot be fixed from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1863 (multiple Class 3 entries PG I/II/III, (D/E)); adr-tool.com UN1863

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) forbidden through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG. See also UN1075 (LPG).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1011 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG. See also UN1075 (LPG).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1978 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adr-tool.com

proper_shipping_name: Petroleum gases, liquefied or Liquefied petroleum gas
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG. Generic LPG entry (butane/propane mixtures).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1075 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1049 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); chemydata.com / dgg.bam.de

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1002 (Class 2.2, (E)); adr-tool.com / nextsds.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic inert gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1006 (Class 2.2, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic gas; no PG. (Distinct from UN1845 solid CO2/dry ice.)
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1013 (Class 2.2, (E)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic inert gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1046 (Class 2.2, (E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

proper_shipping_name: Methane, compressed or Natural gas, compressed with high methane content
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas (CNG); no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1971 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/E: E applies to all carriage; C (tanker/bulk) forbidden through C,D,E. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3159 (Class 2.2, (C/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/E: E applies to all carriage; C (tanker/bulk) forbidden through C,D,E. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1018 (Class 2.2, (C/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/D: D applies to all carriage (forbidden through D,E); C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 8 corrosive subsidiary; no PG. Toxic gas.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1079 (Class 2.3 (8), (C/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/D: D applies to all carriage (forbidden through D,E); C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 8 corrosive subsidiary; no PG. Toxic gas.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1005 (Class 2.3 (8), (C/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/D: D applies to all carriage (forbidden through D,E); C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 5.1 oxidizing and 8 corrosive subsidiaries; no PG. Highly toxic gas.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1017 (Class 2.3 (5.1+8), (C/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

proper_shipping_name: Ethylene oxide or Ethylene oxide with nitrogen up to a total pressure of 1 MPa (10 bar) at 50 C
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 2.1 flammable subsidiary - toxic AND flammable; no PG. Carries additional special provisions.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1040 (Class 2.3 (2.1), (B/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas (stabilized); no PG. Also a recognized carcinogen though ADR classifies as flammable gas.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1086 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. gas. Covers 2.1 (flammable, (B/D)) and 2.2 (non-flammable/non-toxic, (C/E)) depending on the gas. Division and tunnel code depend on the actual gas and cannot be determined from the UN number alone. No PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN3163 (Class 2, division 2.1/2.2 variants); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 2.1 flammable subsidiary. Generic n.o.s.; classification fixed but specific gas not determinable from UN alone. No PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1953 (Class 2.3 (2.1), (B/D)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code E: forbidden through category E only. Class 2.2 non-flammable, non-toxic compressed gas. Generic n.o.s. but classified 2.2 by definition (flammable/toxic take other UN numbers). No PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1956 (Class 2.2, (E)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/E: E applies to all carriage; C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.2 cryogenic inert gas; no PG. Cryogenic handling considerations apply.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1951 (Class 2.2, (C/E))

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/E: E applies to all carriage; C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.2 cryogenic inert gas; no PG. Cryogenic handling considerations apply.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1977 (Class 2.2, (C/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/E: E applies to all carriage; C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.2 with 5.1 oxidizing subsidiary; no PG. Cryogenic AND oxidizing - segregation from flammables required.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1073 (Class 2.2 (5.1), (C/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/E: E applies to all carriage; C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.2 cryogenic gas; no PG. Cryogenic handling considerations apply.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2187 (Class 2.2, (C/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1962 (Class 2.1, (B/D))

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG. See also UN1075 (LPG).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1969 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); cameochemicals ERG

proper_shipping_name: Butadienes, stabilized or Butadienes and hydrocarbon mixture, stabilized, containing more than 20% butadienes
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas (stabilized); no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1010 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas; no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1083 (Class 2.1, (B/D), Transport Cat 2); nextsds.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.1 flammable gas (stabilized, e.g. MAPP P1/P2); no PG.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1060 (Class 2.1, (B/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com / nextsds.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/D: D applies to all carriage (forbidden through D,E); C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 8 corrosive subsidiary; no PG. Reacts with water to release HCl. Toxic gas.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1741 (Class 2.3 (8), (C/D) per ADR toxic-gas pattern)

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code B/D: forbidden through D and E for all carriage; B (tanker/bulk) through B,C,D,E. Class 2.3 toxic gas with 2.1 flammable subsidiary - toxic AND flammable; no PG. Toxic gas.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1064 (Class 2.3 (2.1), (B/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: UN1300 has PG II and PG III entries (by flash-point), both Class 3 tunnel code (D/E). PG depends on flash-point, not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1300 (Class 3, PG II/III, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Representative PG II; also a PG III variant by flash-point/boiling range.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1307 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); nextsds.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1208 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1206 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1262 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1648 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. PG I - very low flash/boiling point; highest flammability in this batch.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1089 (Class 3, PG I, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1175 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Also a recognized carcinogen though ADR classifies as Class 3 flammable liquid PG II.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1114 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

proper_shipping_name: Ethyl methyl ketone or Methyl ethyl ketone
tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. MEK; distinct from UN3105 MEK peroxide (Class 5.2).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1193 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Stabilized to prevent polymerization - inhibitor condition is part of safe carriage.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1247 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1145 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1146 (Class 3, PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com / nextsds.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. PG III - higher flash-point alcohol.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1212 (Class 3, PG III, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. UN1274 has PG II and PG III entries by flash-point; PG and LQ not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1274 (Class 3, PG II/III, (D/E)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. UN1987 has PG II and PG III (plus high-vapour-pressure variant), all Class 3 (D/E). PG/LQ depend on the specific alcohol, not determinable from the UN number alone. A flammable+toxic alcohol takes UN1986 instead.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1987 (Class 3, PG II/III, (D/E)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. UN1989 has PG I, II and III, all Class 3 (D/E) where resolved. PG/LQ depend on the specific aldehyde. A flammable+toxic aldehyde takes UN1988 instead.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1989 (Class 3, PG I/II/III, (D/E))

tunnel_code_meaning: Generic n.o.s. UN1224 has PG II and PG III, both Class 3 (D/E). PG/LQ depend on the specific ketone's flash-point, not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1224 (Class 3, PG II/III, (D/E)); nextsds.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code C/D: D applies to all carriage (forbidden through D,E); C (tanker/bulk) through C,D,E. IMPORTANT: primary Class 6.1 (toxic) with 3 flammable subsidiary, PG I. Despite the 'alcohol' name it is a toxic substance first, not a Class 3 flammable liquid. Highly toxic by inhalation/skin.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1098 (Class 6.1 (3), PG I, (C/D)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid with 6.1 toxic subsidiary, PG II. Toxic subsidiary affects segregation/handling.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1184 (Class 3 (6.1), PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid with 8 corrosive subsidiary, PG II.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1296 (Class 3 (8), PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid PG III. Stabilized to prevent polymerization.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2348 (Class 3, PG III, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid PG III. Stabilized to prevent polymerization.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2055 (Class 3, PG III, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid PG III.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1915 (Class 3, PG III, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid PG III (higher flash-point ketone).
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1110 (Class 3, PG III, (D/E))

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Covers butanol isomers spanning PG II and PG III by flash-point. PG/LQ not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1120 (Class 3, PG II/III, (D/E)); adr-tool.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Covers pentanol isomers spanning PG II and PG III by flash-point. PG/LQ not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1105 (Class 3, PG II/III, (D/E))

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid with 8 corrosive subsidiary. Representative PG II; also a PG III variant by the specific amine.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1106 (Class 3 (8), PG II, (D/E)); adrdangerousgoods.com

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. Class 3 flammable liquid with 6.1 toxic subsidiary, PG II. Toxic subsidiary affects segregation/handling.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN2333 (Class 3 (6.1), PG II, (D/E))

tunnel_code_meaning: Tunnel code D/E: forbidden through E for all carriage; D (tanker/bulk) through D,E. UN1148 has a PG II entry (technically pure >=90%) and a PG III entry (aqueous solution). PG/LQ depend on concentration, not determinable from the UN number alone.
_source: UNECE ADR 2025, Table A, UN1148 (Class 3, PG II/III)
